rationale: The cited paper appears only as part of a grouped introductory literature list in a footnote ("other marketing articles on information pricing") without any specific discussion of its model or findings. The citing paper's conceptual development, hypotheses, and empirical tests are built around Sarvary and Parker (1997), not the 2002 paper on temporal differentiation/second opinions. Because no distinct functional role for this specific citation is shown in the main argument, the citation is best treated as a passing background mention.
